title: alpinia subfusicarpa
intro: Alpinia subfusicarpa, also known as lesser galangal, is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian and Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Alpinia subfusicarpa include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Alpinia subfusicarpa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional cooking and as a flavoring agent.
